_comment: Sample MCP config to plug HTMLook Pro into your AI client (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, etc). Merge this into your client's MCP config file.
_install_steps: 1. HTMLook Pro must be running with MCP server enabled (Settings → MCP → Enable)
2. Note the MCP socket/port shown in HTMLook Pro status bar
3. Add the relevant entry below to your client's mcp.json

htmlook._description: HTMLook Pro embedded MCP server (workspace tools + cite/apply_edit)
htmlook._alternative_via_socket: If HTMLook Pro is already running, point to the socket it announces (path shown in status bar).
